risk_if_not_implemented: Without On-Site Client Segregation, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Physical Access Control
compensating_control_1.description: Physical access control mechanisms exist to enforce physical access authorizations for all physical access points (including designated entry/exit points) to facilities (excluding those areas within the facility officially designated as publicly accessible).
compensating_control_1.justification: Physical Access Control (PES-03) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of On-Site Client Segregation (PES-18) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the facility and physical environment focus of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to ensure network architecture utilizes network segmentation to isolate Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS) to protect from other network resources.
compensating_control_2.justification: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation) (NET-06) provides network-level access restriction that compensates for the absence of On-Site Client Segregation (PES-18) by limiting attacker reach and lateral movement opportunities across the environment. Given the facility and physical environment focus of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
